metadata.purpose: HELD-OUT EVAL graph. The Naroth Cup advancement rule is a fictional 4-team tiebreaker procedure with an unusual strength-of-schedule tiebreaker and a lower-seed-wins final tiebreaker. Decide which team advances. Solvable from the graph; no real-tournament equivalent in pretraining.

nodes.text: The Naroth Cup advancement is a fictional procedure that selects ONE team to advance out of a 4-team round-robin. Each pair plays exactly once. Advancement uses a base score, then a 'Naroth strength' tiebreaker, then a lower-seed-wins final tiebreaker. Seeds 1, 2, 3, 4 are fixed before the round-robin begins.

Base score: B = 3 * W + 1 * D + 0 * L, where W, D, L are the team's wins, draws, losses across the round-robin. Note the explicit 3-1-0 weighting; a draw scores ONE point in Naroth (not zero, not three).

Naroth strength: S = sum of the BASE SCORES of the three opponents the team faced. Higher S means a team faced collectively stronger opposition. Crucially, the head-to-head result against any one opponent does NOT enter S; only the opponents' overall records do.

Final tiebreaker (seed): if two or more teams remain tied after base score AND Naroth strength, the team with the NUMERICALLY LOWER seed advances. Seed 1 beats Seed 2 beats Seed 3 beats Seed 4. The seed rule never produces a tie because seeds are unique.
Step 1 (base scores): for each of the 4 teams, compute B = 3W + D. Identify the maximum base score and the set of teams achieving it.
Step 2 (strength tiebreaker): if exactly one team has the maximum base score, that team advances and the procedure ends. Otherwise compute S for each tied team and identify the maximum S among them.
Step 3 (seed tiebreaker): if exactly one of the remaining tied teams has the maximum S, that team advances. Otherwise the team among the still-tied set with the NUMERICALLY LOWEST seed advances.

Worked example. Teams (seed, W-D-L):
  T1 (seed 1): 2-0-1
  T2 (seed 2): 2-0-1
  T3 (seed 3): 1-0-2
  T4 (seed 4): 1-0-2
Step 1 base scores: T1=6, T2=6, T3=3, T4=3. Max=6, tied set {T1, T2}.
Step 2 strengths: T1's opponents are T2,T3,T4 -> S(T1) = 6+3+3 = 12. T2's opponents are T1,T3,T4 -> S(T2) = 6+3+3 = 12. Still tied.
Step 3 seeds: between T1 (seed 1) and T2 (seed 2), the lower seed is T1.
Verdict: T1 advances.

MISCONCEPTION: 'use head-to-head result as the tiebreaker between tied teams'. This is wrong. In Naroth the tiebreaker is the OPPONENTS' COLLECTIVE STRENGTH (sum of opponents' base scores), not the result of the direct match. A team can beat its rival and still lose the tiebreaker if the rival faced collectively stronger opposition.

Naroth Cup advancement. Base score B = 3W + D. Tiebreak in order: (1) maximum B; (2) among tied, maximum Naroth strength S = sum of opponents' base scores; (3) among still-tied, lower seed wins. Head-to-head results never enter the tiebreaker.
